title: The Iliad implementation lifecycle — pre-review, enrolled sub-system, post-review

authority.vocabulary: docs/mythological-vocabulary.md#The-Iliad (the system-of-systems lifecycle umbrella under which registered components coordinate the creation, governance, maintenance, recovery, evolution, and retirement of enrolled systems)
authority.instituted_by: The Eye's direction, 2026-07-17 (recorded in this delivery); precedent anticipation: AM-40 ('deferred for conscious enrollment later at the Iliad')

steps: MODEL REVIEW: record which model id will fill each seat this run (env-resolved: ANTHROPIC_MODEL/OPENAI_MODEL/XAI_MODEL/GEMINI_MODEL over seats.json role bindings) and note any newly-available model worth a seat change — a seat change itself is a reviewed edit to build-gate/seats.json

CAPABILITY REVIEW: read the task's loadout review (docs/institutional-memory/loadout/TASK-LOADOUTS/) and the prior retrospectives' optimizations; any missing EXTERNAL capability graduates into gate-enforced capability packets on the dossier
record the pre-review as PRE-REVIEWS/<run>.json (template: PRE-REVIEWS/TEMPLATE.json)
phases.verification_note: intent is recorded here; ACTUALITY is verified downstream — per-seat provenance in the run records proves which model actually answered

steps: the implementation is carried per the Argo protocol (comprehension gate -> bounded work -> deterministic verification -> review -> acceptance)
every step logged and verifiable (commit-anchored; a step ledger under docs/runs/<name>/ for multi-step deliveries)
the result is a SUB-SYSTEM enrolled under The Iliad: an entry in CONTRACTS/enrollment.json naming what was created, its evidence, and its memory dirs

retrospective evaluation recorded as RETROSPECTIVES/<run>.json: what was built, verification outcome, what worked, defects found during the work (including process mistakes), and optimizations for future runs
each optimization FEEDS FORWARD: it lands in the relevant TASK-LOADOUTS review, a PRE-REVIEWS template update, or a proposed data/contract change — so the next run's pre-review reads it

NEXT-PHASE EVALUATION VIA DAEDALUS (The Eye's direction, 2026-07-17): the next phase's implementation APPROACH is drafted and matured through a Daedalus workflow — real seats, objection ledger, controller-computed identity — before the next pre-review. The workshop reviews the approach, never the frozen plan (a plan defect still escalates to The Eye); terminal 'submit' makes the matured approach an input to the next pre-review; a stalemate routes to The Eye
an enrollment entry is not 'delivered' without its retrospective (verify-contracts enforces); from slice 5 on, the next pre-review must cite the matured approach (next_phase_workshop)

why: One-shot implementations leak their lessons. The lifecycle makes the loop closed and checkable: the pre-review consumes the last run's retrospective, the implementation is enrolled where The Iliad maintains it, and the post-review writes the next run's inputs. The umbrella term is used with EXACTLY its registered meaning — coordination of enrolled systems' lifecycle, not a new runtime.
change_rule: Phase changes route via CHANGE-PROTOCOL (The Eye). Adding an enrollment or retrospective is the protocol OPERATING, not changing.

oracle.contract_equals_code: docs/institutional-memory/verify-contracts.mjs (enrollment integrity: evidence + memory dirs exist; delivered => retrospective with feed-forward optimizations; post-protocol entries => pre-review recorded; deferred products cross-checked against AM-40 package_roots_exclude; the template exists)
